channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Build a link-preview service with one API call
item.description: When someone pastes a URL into your app's comment box or chat, you want to show a preview card: the page title, a short description, the favicon, and the social image. Doing that yourself means...

item.title: Expected Option<String>, Got Option<()> — Here's Why
item.description: I'm learning Rust and recently ran into something unexpected with Option type. I expected r to be Option<&mut String>, but it came back as Option<()> instead. Here's what I tried: let mut vds =...

item.title: Exponential backoff with jitter stopped our CI retry storms
item.description: TL;DR: Exponential backoff with jitter spreads client retries over time so a recovering service doesn't get flattened by a synchronised wave. We added full jitter to our CI agents and a retry...

item.title: Stop Wasting Bandwidth: Master API Caching with ETags ⚡
item.description: The Redundant Data Tax In data-dense B2B SaaS platforms at Smart Tech Devs, clients constantly poll your API for updates. Imagine a dashboard making a GET /api/system-config request every 60...

item.title: UID2: The Standard That Quietly Replaced the Tracking Cookie
item.description: While the industry celebrated the “death of the third-party cookie,” advertising platforms built a more persistent and standardized identity system based on your email address. Unified ID 2.0...

item.title: _action: Declarative Event Handlers in an Attribute
item.description: Introduction In HTML Traits I went looking for a cleaner way to attach behavior to an element than wrapping it in a custom element. Traits got me most of the way there: composition over...

item.title: HackTheBox: Nexus Writeup
item.description: Executive Summary This writeup documents the complete exploitation chain for the Nexus target system, from initial reconnaissance through root compromise. The attack leveraged: Exposed credentials...

item.title: Snyk launches Evo ADS to secure AI coding agents with real-time governance
item.description: AI coding agents are no longer novelties—they now generate and ship production code, unsupervised, in enterprise environments. Security has not kept pace. Yesterday’s scanners watch code after it...

item.title: Translating Windows system audio in real time — driverless, with no virtual cable
item.description: I build Voxis, an open-source Windows app that translates whatever your system is playing — a video, a game, the other side of a call — and plays the translation back as spoken voice, a few...

item.title: My app didn't go "viral". My AWS bill did.
item.description: And by viral I mean from $0 to $31. Umami told me Clew Directive got 14 visits last month. AWS told me I owed $31 for it. That works out to $2.21 a visitor, which would make it the most expensive...

item.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
item.description: As ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet told TechCrunch in May, what China can currently buy are older-generation deep ultraviolet tools — gear first shipped about a decade ago — the same machines the...

item.title: The top tech Prime Day deals to shop on day two
item.description: Welcome to day two of Amazon’s four-day Prime Day event, which, if we’re being honest, looks a lot like day one. That’s actually good news, though, because many of the best deals are still around,...

item.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
item.description: Backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures, Vishal Sikka’s new venture brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI.

item.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
item.description: In its first earnings report since going public, the AI chipmaker forecast a narrower gross margin in its core business, scaring investors.

item.title: How to Opt Out of Google Search’s New AI Data Training Feature
item.description: Google’s Search history update stores media uploads from your interactions, like images used in reverse image searches, for training its AI models.

item.title: This year’s Prime Day deals on Apple products are the best I’ve seen
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day is now in its second day, and whether you’re looking for a new pair of wireless earbuds or a smartwatch, there’s a good chance you’ll find a discount. The Apple Watch Series 11...

item.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they’re the most resilient
item.description: While AI dominates the layoff narrative, engineers are actually making up a larger share of new hires, according to SignalFire data.

item.title: The 16 best robot vacuum deals available during Prime Day
item.description: If you’ve been wanting to buy a robot vacuum but have been put off by how much it can cost to get a good one, now is not a bad time to start looking. We’re now on day two of Prime Day, and Amazon,...

item.title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
item.description: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are leaving Google for Anthropic, following departures from top scientists Noam Shazeer and John Jumper.

item.title: Charlie Kirk’s legacy is a 30-year sentence for moving zines
item.description: Just days after a gunman killed conservative activist Charlie Kirk, it became clear that President Donald Trump would use the assassination to fuel a crackdown on free speech. To avenge Kirk's...

item.title: Microsoft introduces cheaper Surface devices with half the memory
item.description: Microsoft just added a cheaper 12-inch Surface Pro and 13-inch Surface Laptop to its lineup. Both models come equipped with 8GB of RAM instead of 16GB, costing $849 for the specced-down Surface...

item.title: The memory chip crunch is paying off for this US company
item.description: Revenue quadrupled to $41.45 billion compared with the same period a year ago. The company's profit, meanwhile, rose from $1.88 billion to an incredible $28.2 billion year-over-year.

item.title: A24 Knows You’re Mad About the Google AI Collab
item.description: Indie movie fans are upset about Google DeepMind’s $75 million investment in the studio, which comes as AI companies are deepening their influence in Hollywood.

item.title: One-two punch delivered in global operation disrupts cybercrime "assembly line"
item.description: "Operation Endgame" simultaneously disrupts two widely used crime tools.

item.title: A new paper argues Microsoft exaggerated its quantum claims a year ago
item.description: A critique published in Nature Wednesday calls the basic technology behind Microsoft's "breakthrough" quantum computing chip the Majorana 1 into question. Microsoft unveiled the chip in February...

item.title: Companies are scrambling to stop employees from maxing out AI budgets with small tasks
item.description: The tokenmaxxing era was brief. We now appear to be entering the era of token rationing.

item.title: Congresswoman denies staff used AI to write defense funding amendment
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-FL) says her staff used AI for "spellcheck" in an amendment summary for a major defense bill, but denies it was used for the bill text itself and says "NO Legislation is...

item.title: I Met With China’s Top AI Experts. They’re Freaking Out, Too
item.description: The AI arms race between China and the US has researchers on both sides worried about a “Chernobyl moment.”

item.title: GTA VI is a worrying sign for the future of physical games
item.description: Rockstar Games has finally given Grand Theft Auto VI a price ahead of the game's November 19th launch. But while announcing that the game would cost $79.99, Rockstar also confirmed that the...

item.title: Google is finally opening the Play Store to outside payments
item.description: While the court still hasn't signed off on the massive settlement resolving Epic's antitrust lawsuit against Google for having a monopoly over Android's app store with Google Play, the tech giant...
